dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a breathing problem call at Apt #D in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Taylor, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I was spraying Lysol in my car and started having trouble breathing. I took albuterol, but it didn't help.
Can you tell me your name and where you are?
I can see you're clearly aware and looking at me through this whole conversation.
Please say the sky is blue and the grass is green for me, if you can, okay?
Touch your nose with your finger, then your toes with your toe. How's that feeling?.
Are you having any trouble breathing when you speak in full sentences?
You seem to be in moderate respiratory distress. Let me listen to your lungs a moment.
I'm going to take your pulse and blood pressure now to get a baseline.
Your pulse is 138-this is quite fast. You are tachycardic right now.
Your breathing is 20 breaths a minute. It's a bit rapid and shallow for comfort.
Your blood pressure is 238 over 144. That's quite high.
Your oxygen saturation is 93%. We need to watch this over time closely.
Are there any concerns with hand or foot temperature and cap refill?
My hands and feet feel warm enough, and they don't have a bluish color.
I detect tachypnea with labored breathing using short phrases. No use of accessory muscles observed.
The EKG is showing sinus tachycardia. Let me perform a 12-lead shortly for more detail.
No perfusion changes reported yet. Skin appears normally warm and dry.
Your eyes look alert. Your pupils are equal and reactive.
I don't see any swelling or tenderness in your neck area for now.
I also do not see any signs of jugular venous distension in your neck at the moment.
So, have you had any chest pain or tightness in your chest today?.
The chest pain started during the ride. It feels like my chest is caving in.
How long have you had this chest sensation? Any radiation into your arms or legs?.
It started recently. It is new and sharp, and it does not go anywhere else right now.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad is the chest pressure for you right now?.
It's a 6 right now.
Your skin is warm, dry, and appears normal in color.
Tell me, do you have a history of asthma or any other chronic condition?.
Yes, I have mild asthma, plus diabetes and high blood pressure.
What medications are you currently taking to manage these?.
I take lisinopril for the high blood pressure daily.
Do you have any allergies to medications or substances in your history?.
No, I don't have any known drug allergies in my medical records.
What were you doing just before the difficulty breathing began?.
I was in my car, spraying Lysol to disinfect it, and that triggered the trouble breathing.
Did the problem come on suddenly during rest or while you were active?.
It was at rest-just regular cleansing in the car when it started.
Your airway is unobstructed from mouth to throat inside. No internal blockage signs observed.
Let me check your chest for wheezing or crackles. I hear clear air movement bilaterally right now.
I feel your stomach is soft with no signs of tenderness under light pressure.
I'll check your legs now. Good peripheral pulses are detected along both arms and legs.
I see no deformities, edema, or muscle swelling right now in the extremities.
I'm going to connect a four-lead EKG now for ongoing heart monitoring.
The EKG is still showing a sinus tach. Performing a 12-lead for more insights now.
We are giving albuterol and atrovent treatments through a nebulizer to help ease your breathing.
Your SpO2 is up to 97%. That's a good response so far to the treatments we provided.
Your respiratory rate is now up to 29-you still seem to be working pretty hard.
You're tachypneic right now at 32 breaths per minute-I will monitor it closely during transport.
Pulse remains elevated at 132, still in a tachycardic range.
Your blood pressure is 222 over 135. It's still very high but trending slightly.
Let's recheck your BP and pulse now to ensure we have stable trends.
Pulse remains at 132. Blood pressure is 222 over 135-tending to stay consistent.
Your SpO2 is 99 now. Your oxygenation is improving.
Let me also check your end-tidal CO2 level now, which reads 28-as a baseline for en route.
An IV has been placed-20g in your left hand.
We'll place you onto a stretcher and secure you for a safe ride to the hospital.
We plan to take you to [COMPANY_NAME] for further evaluation. Would that work?
Yes, I prefer [CITY].
All of your baseline vitals, the EKG results, and the medications we gave have been documented.
Hospital staff will be informed of your medications and the change in chest symptoms during the ride.
I will be handing them the [UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER] data before we transfer care.
You'll be moved from the stretcher to the hospital bed independently under your care.
Your time in the bathroom will be noted and shared with the hospital team.
Due to your chest pain and EKG findings, we will transport you to [CITY] for further evaluation.
We are now en route. You're secured and with stable vitals at the moment.
Nurses in the ER will provide further assistance once you arrive at the hospital.
We'll stay with you during the transfer and until you're ready to be handed over to triage.
Thank you for your cooperation, and we'll ensure you get the best care once you arrive.
